scatter_add_math.formula: dKV[g, d] = sum_{r: group_id[r] = g} partial[r, d]
scatter_add_math.equivalent_linear_algebra_view: A^T P, where A is a sparse 0/1 route matrix with one nonzero per partial row
scatter_add_math.elementwise_read: Each partial row is vector-added across D/head dims into the destination compressed group; the expensive part is the indexed reduction/atomic accumulation, not per-element arithmetic.
scatter_add_math.order_dependency: Mathematically order independent. GPU floating-point atomics are not bitwise order deterministic, but any order is valid within the numeric tolerance contract after the producer has generated the required partial rows.

read: The dKV ScatterAdd is not raw matrix multiply; it is the backward of a gather/routing operation, equivalent to multiplying compact partial rows by the transpose of a sparse route matrix.
For the retained 32x512 candidate, tile-local grouping could in theory reduce global dKV writes by 23.813x, but the natural producer order has nearly no adjacent same-group runs.
A run-length-only detached ScatterAdd or simple reorder-free consumer cannot recover the coalescing target.
The next kernel rewrite should preserve the query-major MFMA/dot schedule while adding explicit tile-local hash, segmented, or sorted grouping before global dKV writes.
commands: docker run --rm --entrypoint python --device=/dev/kfd --device=/dev/dri --group-add video --ipc=host --network=host --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE --security-opt seccomp=unconfined -e HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 -e PYTHONPATH=/workspace/tools/dsv4_runtime_probe:/workspace/sources/wip/torchtitan -v /scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527:/workspace -w /workspace onenexus/nexus-titan:rocm722-pytorch-nightly-mori tools/dsv4_runtime_probe/analyze_sparse_mla_shared_kv_actual_li_route_order.py --seq-len 4096 --layer-id 2 --token-blocks 16,32,64 --selected-blocks 128,256,512 --summary-path /workspace/run_artifacts/actual_li_route_order_analysis_s4096_layer2_tb16-64_sb128-512_20260603.json --json
